name: Bhai Charanjit Singh (Patiala)
body: Bhai Charanjit Singh (Patiala) was born into a family of raagis in the year 1940 in Nankana Sahib, Pakistan. He was exposed to music at a very young age by his grandfather, Bhai Teja Singh and father, Bhai Harnam Singh. In the initial years of his training, he learned how to play the tabla from Bhai Bahadur Singh (Jalandhar) and then went on to learn from Ustad Kirpal Singh (Patiala) for 15 years. Before forming his own jatha, he played tabla with many renowned raagis such as Bhai Pal Singh, Bhai Santa Singh Kanwal, Bhai Gurnam Singh (Delhi), Bhai Kartar Singh Parwana, Sant Sujan Singh, Bhai Thakur Singh Almast and many more. His interest in singing led him to learn Kirtan from Bhai Pal Singh who then encouraged him to form his own jatha. Currently, Bhai Charanjit Singh Ji’s jatha spans three generations. His son, Bhai Sukhwinder Singh accompanies him on the side harmonium and vocals and his grandson, Bhai Gagandeep Singh plays the tabla. They are keeping the rababi tradition alive today by performing many old compositions previously sung at Nankana Sahib by renowned rababis such as Bhai Jaswant Singh & Pal Singh (Jasa-Pala). Bhai Charanjit Singh Ji lived in Bombay for many years before moving back to Patiala. If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Charanjit Singh (Patiala), please contact us.

Update: Bhai Sahib Ji passed away peacefully at 3 a.m. today, the 26th of August 2020. He had gone to sleep last night after returning home from a Kirtan program and awoke at 3 a.m. due to a drop in his blood sugar level. He bid his family farewell and calmly said it was his time to leave before taking his last breath.

The recordings below were contributed by Sahib Singh Bakshi, who also has his own channel on YouTube. Please visit his channel at the link below for more treasures https://www.youtube.com/user/sahibsingh100
